issues.description: FR-3b JSON schemas for approve/cancel/resume specify `previous_state` and `new_state` fields, but the server API does not return these. The actual API responses contain `state` + `approve_requested`/`cancel_requested`/`resume_requested` booleans (see cli/src/commands/approve.rs, cancel.rs, resume.rs). The CLI cannot know the previous state without an extra round-trip, which introduces a race condition.
issues.suggestion: Either (a) change the JSON schemas to match the actual API response fields (`state`, `approve_requested`, etc.), (b) explicitly state the CLI should make a status query before the action to capture previous_state (and accept the race), or (c) note that the server API needs to be updated to return previous_state/new_state in the response body.

issues.description: FR-3b `nemo models --json` schema uses `"name": "anthropic"` but the codebase consistently uses `"claude"` as the provider name (see cli/src/commands/models.rs, auth.rs). The schema also includes a `roles` field (`["implementer", "reviewer"]`) per model, but no role-to-model assignment exists in the codebase â€” any model can serve any role. The models command uses hardcoded model lists (CLAUDE_MODELS, OPENAI_MODELS constants) and credential status checks, which don't map to the spec's schema.
issues.suggestion: Update the models JSON schema to use `"claude"` instead of `"anthropic"` for the provider name. Either drop the `roles` field or redefine it as the set of roles the model *can* serve (which is always all roles). Consider whether the JSON should also include credential status (local/cluster/valid) since that's the primary value of the models command.

issues.description: FR-4c introduces `--no-hints` to suppress recovery hints, but doesn't specify whether it's a global flag (like `--server` and `--insecure`) or per-command. Since error hints can appear on any command that hits the API, it should logically be global. The spec also doesn't address where in the code the hint system intercepts errors â€” the current flow uses `anyhow::bail!("API error ({status}): {body}")` in client.rs, and hints need an integration point (client layer, command layer, or main error display).
issues.suggestion: Specify that `--no-hints` is a global flag on the `Cli` struct. Describe the integration point: either (a) a wrapper around client methods that catches errors and appends hints before re-raising, or (b) a top-level error handler in main() that inspects the anyhow error string and appends hints before printing to stderr.

issues.description: FR-3b `nemo extend --json` schema uses field names `previous_rounds`, `new_rounds`, and `state` but the actual API response (ExtendResponse in extend.rs) returns `prior_max_rounds`, `new_max_rounds`, and `resumed_to_state`. The semantic difference matters: `new_rounds` (20) vs `new_max_rounds` (20) is the same value, but the spec's `state` field implies current state while the API's `resumed_to_state` is the state the loop was resumed into.
issues.suggestion: Decide whether the CLI JSON output should mirror API field names (simpler, less confusion) or translate them (friendlier but creates a mapping layer). Document whichever choice is made. If translating, explicitly list the mapping.

issues.description: FR-3b `nemo auth --json` schema omits `ssh` as a provider, but `nemo auth` pushes ssh credentials by default alongside claude and openai. The schema also assumes a clean per-provider status/message structure, but the actual auth flow has complex branching (keychain fallback for claude, OAuth bundle normalization for openai, file existence checks) that produces multiple warning/error messages per provider, not a single message string.
issues.suggestion: Include `ssh` in the auth JSON schema example. Consider whether `message` should be an array of strings to capture multi-line feedback (e.g., 'disk credentials stale; using fresh keychain entry'), or whether the JSON output should be a simplified summary that drops the detailed diagnostics.

issues.description: The custom help subcommand implementation section says `help <cmd>` 'falls back to rendering the matching subcommand's long_about' but doesn't describe the mechanism. Since clap's built-in help subcommand is disabled, the custom implementation needs to programmatically access other subcommands' metadata (e.g., via `Cli::command().find_subcommand(cmd)`). This is feasible but non-trivial â€” the implementer needs to know whether to use clap's Command API or maintain a separate help text registry.
issues.suggestion: Add an implementation note stating that `help <cmd>` should use `Cli::command().find_subcommand(name)` to retrieve the subcommand's `Command` object and render its help via `cmd.render_long_help()` or equivalent clap API. This makes the mechanism explicit.

issues.description: FR-1a mentions `nemo start spec.md` in workflow examples but the actual start command reads a local file path (spec_path argument). The current implementation (start.rs) reads the file content and sends it as the request body. The spec's workflow example is fine, but the recovery playbook for 404 says 'Spec must exist on the repo's default branch, or be uploaded via --local-spec'. There is no `--local-spec` flag â€” the spec_path IS the local file. The error hint references a nonexistent flag.
issues.suggestion: Remove the `--local-spec` reference from the 404 recovery hint. The correct hint should reference the actual `spec_path` argument behavior: 'Ensure the spec file exists at the given path. Run `nemo start --help` for usage.'

issues.description: FR-1a recovery playbook text contains what appears to be a template rendering artifact: `nemo auth --claude {{SPEC}}{{SPEC}} nemo resume <id>`. The `{{SPEC}}` doubled text is likely corruption. The intended text is two separate commands: `nemo auth --claude` then `nemo resume <id>`.
issues.suggestion: Fix to: 'AWAITING_REAUTH â†’ `nemo auth --claude` then `nemo resume <id>`' or use a bullet list for the two commands.

issues.description: FR-2c lists all commands that need long_about but omits `capabilities` (the new command being added by FR-6). It also doesn't mention the `help` subcommand itself, though that one arguably doesn't need long_about since it IS the help system.
issues.suggestion: Add `capabilities` to the FR-2c list. Optionally note that the `help` subcommand itself doesn't need a custom long_about.

issues.description: FR-6b implementation note says 'The commands array is derived from the clap Command definition at build time (iterate subcommands)'. This is runtime iteration over `Cli::command().get_subcommands()`, not build-time. The distinction matters because build-time implies a build script or proc macro.
issues.suggestion: Change 'at build time' to 'at runtime' â€” the clap Command tree is constructed at runtime via the derive macro's generated code, and subcommands are iterated then.
